story.iWant: her module için ayrı seeder class'ı
story.soThat: RoleSeeder, PermissionSeeder, UserSeeder, FileSeeder ayrı ayrı çalışabilsin
story.tasks: 21 tasks across 7 main areas covering RoleSeeder, PermissionSeeder, UserSeeder, FileSeeder, SmsSeeder, idempotent operations, and transaction support

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 7 acceptance criteria: RoleSeeder with upsert operations, PermissionSeeder with role assignments, UserSeeder with password hashing, FileSeeder with sample files, SmsSeeder with Turkish phone format, all seeders idempotent, and transaction support

doc.title: Epic 13: Advanced Seeder Infrastructure Tech Spec

docs.doc: Defines seeder structure with 5 individual seeder classes in prisma/seeders/, static seed() methods, and integration with factories. Specifies idempotent operations and transaction support.

Complete story definition with acceptance criteria for 5 seeders (Role, Permission, User, File, SMS), idempotent operations using upsert() and createMany(skipDuplicates: true), and transaction support.
Testing pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert for seeders. Jest framework for unit/integration tests, separate test database setup, and cleanup between tests. Minimum 70% coverage enforced by CI/CD.

doc.title: Story 13.1: Prisma Native Seeding Entry Point

docs.doc: Main seed.ts entry point structure with async main() function, seeder execution order (roles → permissions → users), and console logging with ✓ checkmarks.

doc.title: Story 13.2: Model Factory Pattern & Faker Integration

docs.doc: Factory pattern foundation with 4 factory classes (User, Role, Permission, File) using Faker.js v8+ with Turkish locale. Static generate() and generateMany() methods with Prisma type safety.

code.artifact: Already implemented with upsert operations for admin, staff, user, guest roles. Uses idempotent upsert() with domainID_name composite key. Console logging with ✓ indicators.
Create permissions and assign to roles via RolePermission entities. Should use $transaction for role-permission assignments.
Create admin, staff, and regular users. Should import and use UserFactory from Story 13.2 and hashPassword utility from Story 13.6.
Seed sample S3 files (images, PDFs, videos) with file metadata (size, mimeType, fileName). Should use FileFactory for data generation.
Already implemented for SMS logs with OTP codes and Turkish phone numbers (05xx format). Generate realistic SMS delivery statuses.
Generates Turkish user data with 05xx phone format, Turkish names, and realistic emails. Uses Prisma.UserCreateInput type safety with override support.
Generate role data with English names (admin, staff, user, guest). Static generate() and generateMany() methods.
Generate permissions with module-action combinations (auth.users.*, files.*, etc.).
Produces realistic file metadata with Turkish filenames, proper MIME types, and varied file sizes.
Database schema with User, Role, Permission, File, SMS entities. Defines data models for seeder implementations.

dependencies.dep: Prisma ORM client for database operations. Used by all seeders for CRUD operations.
Password hashing utility. Required by UserSeeder for hashing test user passwords (Story 13.6).
Faker.js library for realistic test data generation. Used by factories for Turkish locale data (names, phones, emails).
TypeScript execution engine. Required for running seeders via `npm run prisma:seed`.
Testing framework. Used for unit and integration tests of seeder classes.

constraints.constraint: All seeders must use static seed(prisma: PrismaClient) method pattern for compatibility with seed.ts entry point
Idempotent operations required: upsert() for single records, createMany(skipDuplicates: true) for bulk operations
Console logging with ✓ success indicators for each seeder operation
TypeScript strict mode enabled (Epic 12.4) - all seeders must maintain type safety with Prisma schema types
Import organization follows 8-category pattern from Epic 12.5: External libraries → DTOs → Services → Repositories → Entities → Interfaces → Enums → Events
Transaction support using $transaction() for maintaining data consistency across related entities
Turkish locale data required: 05xx phone format, Turkish names, realistic emails for test data
Domain-based multi-tenancy: All entities include domainID field for tenant isolation

interfaces.interface: Standard seeder contract: All seeder classes must implement static seed() method accepting PrismaClient and returning Promise<void>
Generate single user with Turkish data, Prisma type-safe, supports override pattern

interface.name: UserFactory.generateMany()
interface.signature: static generateMany(count: number, overrides?: Partial<Prisma.UserCreateInput>): Prisma.UserCreateInput[]

interfaces.interface: Generate array of users with bulk data generation capability
Upsert operation for idempotent role creation using composite unique key
Bulk create permissions with duplicate skipping for idempotent seeding

tests.standards: Testing pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA) for all seeder tests. Jest framework with separate test database. Setup: beforeAll connect Prisma, beforeEach seed test data, afterEach cleanup (deleteMany), afterAll disconnect. Minimum 70% coverage required. Integration tests validate full seeding workflow with transaction rollback.
tests.locations: test/seeders/*.spec.ts for unit tests, test/integration/*.spec.ts for E2E tests, test/factories/*.spec.ts for factory tests. Glob patterns: "test/**/*.{test,spec}.ts" for all tests. Seeders tested with mocked PrismaClient, factories tested with actual Faker.js output validation.

idea.description: Test RoleSeeder.seed() creates all 4 roles (admin, staff, user, guest) with upsert idempotency - run twice and verify no duplicates
Test PermissionSeeder creates all permissions and assigns to roles via $transaction - verify RolePermission junction table populated
Test UserSeeder uses UserFactory and hashPassword utility - verify Turkish users created with proper role assignments
Test FileSeeder uses FileFactory - verify sample images, PDFs created with realistic metadata (size, mimeType, fileName)
Test SmsSeeder generates Turkish phone numbers (05xx format) and OTP codes - verify phone format compliance
Test all seeders idempotent - run each seeder twice and verify no duplicate records created
Test seed.ts $transaction wraps all seeders - simulate failure and verify rollback works
